appliedUpdates.description: Added Ring Allreduce section documenting: algorithm design (FP32 scratch for precision), performance results (ring ~8.5x slower than star for 10KB payloads on PCIe), correctness results (max_abs_err=0.0 vs CPU FP32 ref), and design decision to keep star topology as default.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add note: When implementing alternative communication topologies (ring vs star allreduce, etc.), workers should benchmark against the baseline BEFORE implementing and document expected performance trade-offs. For this project's PCIe topology (all GPUs 2 hops apart, hidden_size=5120 = 10KB), ring allreduce is approximately 8.5x slower than star topology due to Python-synchronized sequential P2P rounds. Workers should check whether the payload size justifies the topology change.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: ring-allreduce-kernel measured ring=1015 us vs star=119 us for 10KB payload. ring-allreduce-integration measured star=26.0 tok/s vs ring=7.7 tok/s (3.38x slower end-to-end). Despite this, the feature was correctly implemented with proper documentation of the trade-off.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add note: The 'async' naming in allreduce_residual_async() for RingAllreduce is misleading — the ring allreduce blocks the CPU during 6 rounds of stream_synchronize() calls (18 total sync calls for TP=4). Workers extending ring allreduce should be aware that the 'async' benefit is only the event-gated start, not true non-blocking execution. Only P2PAllreduce.allreduce_residual_async() achieves true non-blocking behavior (all GPU work submitted without CPU waiting).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Code review of RingAllreduce._run_ring_allreduce() shows stream_synchronize() called between each of 6 P2P rounds, blocking the Python thread. This makes allreduce_residual_async effectively synchronous for ring topology.

rejectedObservations.reason: This is a minor VRAM waste (chunk_size × 2 bytes per GPU × 4 GPUs = ~10KB total). The implementation correctly transitioned to FP32 P2P transfers. Not blocking and low severity — fix can happen during cleanup in a future milestone.

rejectedObservations.observation: Test benchmark uses cached+stream for ring vs star comparison (not C dispatch path)
rejectedObservations.reason: The benchmark is testing allreduce topology overhead in isolation, which is correct when using cached+stream. C dispatch comparison is a separate test. The benchmark design is intentional and documented in the test.

keyMetrics.val_ring_004: NOT MET (expected - ring slower for small payloads)
